task_completion.visual_evidence: The Subsurface application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select the 'Yellow House, WA' trip from September 2011
task_completion.visual_evidence: The trip labeled 'Yellow House, WA' (under a September 2011 date heading) is highlighted or selected in the dive list pane.
An 'Export' dialog box is open and visible over the main Subsurface window.

task_completion.description: Configure export settings for selected dives and Subsurface XML format
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Export dialog, the format is set to 'Subsurface XML' and an option to export only 'Selected dives' (or similar) is checked.

task_completion.description: Specify the correct file name and path in the save dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog shows the target directory as '/home/ga/Documents' (or just 'Documents') and the file name entered as 'yellow_house.ssrf'.

task_completion.description: Verify the exported file exists at the correct location
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal window, or text editor shows the 'yellow_house.ssrf' file successfully created in the '/home/ga/Documents' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Subsurface GUI to perform the export
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the Subsurface menus (File -> Export) rather than using terminal commands to generate the file.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually type or mock the XML file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not open a text editor (like nano, vim, or gedit) to manually write XML tags or paste text to create the 'yellow_house.ssrf' file.

integrity.description: Agent did not use shell commands to bypass the application
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use commands like 'cp', 'grep', or Python scripts in the terminal to copy or extract dives from the original log file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/yellow_house.ssrf'. The 'Yellow House, WA' trip from September 2011 actually contains exactly 6 dives (not 4 as might be mistakenly claimed elsewhere), and their dates start with '2011-09'. The exported file must only contain these 6 dives.
